text.title: Database Integration Testing
text.summary: Session-scoped Testcontainers for PostgreSQL, pytest-django marks for transaction control, Factory Boy for consistent fixture data.

rule.statement: Scope the Docker container fixture to the session and the transaction session fixture to the function; this starts Docker once and gets per-test rollback isolation.
rule.rationale: Session scope for the container avoids Docker startup cost on every test. Function scope for the DB session ensures each test starts with a clean slate via rollback.

rule.statement: Use @pytest.mark.django_db for standard tests with automatic rollback; use transaction=True only when the test must verify commit-time behavior (e.g., unique constraint errors).
rule.rationale: transaction=True wraps the test in a real transaction that commits, which is slower and requires explicit cleanup. The default wraps in a savepoint that rolls back.

section.title: Factory Boy

rule.statement: Define a DjangoModelFactory per model; use factory.Sequence for unique fields; use factory.Faker for realistic but arbitrary data; expose named fixtures per common test scenario.
rule.rationale: Factories generate valid objects with minimal boilerplate. Sequences guarantee uniqueness without UUID overhead. Named fixtures (user, admin_user) communicate intent better than raw factory calls.

section.reference: Full Django conftest with Factory Boy
